title: Windows PrivEsc Methodology
when_to_use: After gaining standard Windows user access

steps.title: Initial Setup/Scanning
steps.instruction: Run WinPEAS executable to perform automated Windows privilege escalation enumeration
steps.tip: Start with the most reliable information source for windows privesc methodology
steps.expected_output: Comprehensive report highlighting privilege escalation vectors and misconfigurations

steps.title: Data Collection/Configuration
steps.instruction: Enumerate services with wmic to identify services with modifiable paths
steps.tip: Organize data systematically to avoid duplicates and ensure coverage
steps.expected_output: List of services with writable binary paths or parameters for DLL injection

steps.title: Analysis/Enumeration
steps.instruction: Use accesschk to verify file permissions on sensitive directories and services
steps.tip: Look for patterns and correlations in collected data
steps.expected_output: Confirmed writable access to system directories or service files

steps.title: Advanced Technique/Filtering
steps.instruction: Run Invoke-AllChecks PowerUp function to identify escalation opportunities
steps.tip: Use multiple techniques to increase coverage and accuracy
steps.expected_output: PowerShell output listing all discovered privilege escalation vulnerabilities

steps.title: Correlation/Testing
steps.instruction: Check AlwaysInstallElevated registry setting for MSI installation escalation
steps.tip: Validate findings across different tools and methods
steps.expected_output: Confirmed registry misconfiguration enabling elevated MSI installation

steps.title: Validation/Exploitation
steps.instruction: Enumerate token privileges with 'whoami /priv' looking for SeImpersonate
steps.expected_output: List of user token privileges including service account escalation opportunities

steps.title: Documentation/Reporting
steps.instruction: Search for stored credentials in registry, files, and configuration databases
steps.tip: Review all findings before compilation for accuracy and completeness
steps.expected_output: Recovered plaintext or recoverable credentials for escalation attempts
